heading: Art. 118. Murder
label: 918 Art. 118. Murder
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)91850:712.(Art. 118), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 140The words “of this section” are omitted as surplusage.

Amendments2023— substituted “such person” for “he” in introductory and concluding provisions and substituted “direct, unless such person is otherwise sentenced in accordance with a plea agreement entered into between the parties under (article 53a).” for “ direct.” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 118–31section 853a of this title2016—Par. (4). struck out “forcible sodomy,” after “burglary,”.Pub. L. 114–3282014—Par. (4). substituted “forcible sodomy” for “sodomy”.Pub. L. 113–2912011—Par. (4). substituted “sexual assault, sexual assault of a child, aggravated sexual contact, sexual abuse of a child,” for “aggravated sexual assault, aggravated sexual assault of a child, aggravated sexual contact, aggravated sexual abuse of a child, aggravated sexual contact with a child,”.Pub. L. 112–812006—Par. (4). substituted “rape, rape of a child, aggravated sexual assault, aggravated sexual assault of a child, aggravated sexual contact, aggravated sexual abuse of a child, aggravated sexual contact with a child,” for “rape,”.Pub. L. 109–1631992—Par. (3). substituted “another” for “others”.Pub. L. 102–484

children.text: has a premeditated design to kill;
intends to kill or inflict great bodily harm;
is engaged in an act which is inherently dangerous to another and evinces a wanton disregard of human life; or

is engaged in the perpetration or attempted perpetration of burglary, rape, rape of a child, sexual assault, sexual assault of a child, aggravated sexual contact, sexual abuse of a child, robbery, or aggravated arson;
